title: Project Wrap-Up & Inference
concept: Testing the model on custom sentences to observe sentiment classification. 'Mini Project: Sentiment Analyzer is complete!'.

exercises.prompt: What metrics did we use to evaluate our sentiment classifier in the last lesson?
exercises.explain: F1-score, precision, and recall help us understand how well our model is classifying sentiment.

cards.title: Let's Wrap Up!
cards.body: In this lesson, we'll learn how to test our sentiment analyzer on new sentences. Are you ready to see it in action?

cards.title: What is Inference?
cards.body: Inference is when we use a trained model to make predictions on new data. We will observe how our model classifies the sentiment of fresh sentences.

cards.title: Like a Detective!
cards.body: Think of inference like a detective examining new clues. Based on what they learned previously, they determine whether the new clues suggest something positive, negative, or neutral.

cards.title: Testing Our Model
cards.body: For example, if we test the sentence 'I love this product!', the model should classify it as 'positive'. Let's see how it performs on different sentences!

cards.title: Myth: Only Training is Important
cards.body: Some may think training the model is all that matters. However, testing the model on new sentences is crucial to ensure it works well in real situations.

cards.title: Model Inference
cards.body: In the left side, we have our trained model. On the right, we see new sentences to analyze.

exercises.prompt: What do we call the process of testing our model on new sentences?
exercises.explain: Inference is the step where we apply our trained model to new data to see how well it works.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Inference is when we retrain our sentiment analyzer on the same data.
exercises.explain: Inference is about making predictions on new data, not retraining on the same data.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Testing a model on new sentences is known as ____.
exercises.sentence: Testing a model on new sentences is known as inference.
exercises.explain: Inference is the correct term for predicting sentiments based on the trained model.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about inference is made by the AI?

options.text: Inference is where we create new sentences.
Inference allows us to see how well our model predicts sentiment on new data.
Inference means retraining the analysis on existing data set.

exercises.explain: The correct statement explains that inference involves using the model to predict on new sentences, not creating new sentences or retraining.

exercises.prompt: Why is it important to test our sentiment analysis model on new sentences?

options.text: To ensure it works only on perfect data.
To see how well it performs in real-world scenarios.
To increase our confusion about the model's abilities.
To avoid using any data in the evaluation process.

exercises.explain: We need to test our model on new sentences to confirm it can accurately classify sentiment in different contexts. This ensures that our model is effective in real-world applications. Mini Project: Sentiment Analyzer is complete!
